examples: The forest seemed interminable; nowhere did he discover a break in it, not even a woodman's road. He had not known that he lived in so wild a region.
But these interminable situations do not include the millions more uprooted people who are displaced within their own countries and who far outnumber the world's refugees.
But could an Abe-led government really snap Japan out of its seemingly interminable economic malaise?
Instead, it's full of stream-of-consciousness ramblings and interminable descriptions of the grim Canvey landscape.
